hostMessages.title: Round 2: The Timeline
hostMessages.text: Detectives, Round 2 is live. Now the case shifts from shock to structure. Rebuild the timeline. Who called Lena, who crossed paths with her, and who appeared where they should not have been? The truth is tightening around movement, not rumor.

hostMessages.title: Case Closed
hostMessages.text: Detectives, the case is closed. Lena Broussard was murdered by Owen Landry. His motive was simple greed wrapped in panic: Lena had discovered he was skimming sponsor money and preparing to expose him before the final toast. To silence her, he tampered with the drink exchange and then hid her emergency injector to make the death look like a terrible accident. But the cover-up broke apart under pressure. The camera gap, the fake sponsor invoices, the office safe log, and Lena’s unsent warning all point in the same direction. The bar was full of noise, but the timeline never lied.

breakdown.prompt: How was the murder carried out?
breakdown.selectedLabel: Switched Lena's allergy-safe drink and hid her injector
breakdown.correctLabel: Switched Lena's allergy-safe drink and hid her injector

breakdown.selectedLabel: To hide the stolen sponsor money
breakdown.correctLabel: To hide the stolen sponsor money

breakdown.prompt: Which evidence best proves the case?
breakdown.selectedLabel: Camera export gap, safe access log, and fake sponsor invoices
breakdown.correctLabel: Camera export gap, safe access log, and fake sponsor invoices
